generatedDirectories.purpose: Locally runnable production artifact produced by npm run build
Ignored local build and server caches

commandsActuallyRun.details: The supplied outer worktree is not a Jujutsu repository. No product file was affected.
The supplied outer worktree is not a Jujutsu repository.
Initialized the local project and installed dependencies inside repos/daymark.
Local development server started at http://localhost:3001 and was stopped after implementation.
Removed unused database and styling starter dependencies.
Run four times during implementation; every run passed all 9 tests. The final run passed 9, failed 0, skipped 0.
The first run found one React effect issue. The hydration update was deferred safely; the next three runs passed with zero findings.
The first run exposed unused starter Cloudflare/D1 types. The unused database scaffold was removed and worker types were narrowed; the next three runs passed.
Run four times during implementation; every run completed and the final artifact is in dist/.
The built production server was started locally after each of three final build stages, then stopped after its health check.
Run three times against the production artifact; each response was HTTP 200.

passedChecks: Local calendar-day rollover with prior-day removal
Malformed JSON and invalid-shape storage recovery
correctedCheckFailures: An initial lint check found a synchronous hydration state update inside an effect; this was corrected before final verification.
An initial TypeScript check found unused starter-only Cloudflare/D1 types; the unused database scaffold was removed and final type checks pass.

skippedChecks.check: Automated browser interaction, screenshot, and visual-regression testing
skippedChecks.reason: Not required by the frozen proof list; no browser automation dependency was added. Responsive and focus treatments are implemented in CSS but were not independently visually reviewed.
Explicitly outside the allowed stopping point.

knownLimitations: Persistence is intentionally limited to one browser profile on one device and is lost if that browser's site data is cleared.

There is no automated end-to-end browser or visual-regression suite; behavior is covered at the state/storage layer and the final app passes lint, type checking, build, and production HTTP health checks.

The package manager reported 11 dependency-tree audit advisories after cleanup (2 low, 3 moderate, 6 high); npm audit remediation was not run because forced upgrades could change the frozen dependency stack.
Vinext reports the single app route as unclassified by its current static analyzer during build; the build completes and the production route returns HTTP 200.
externalActionsNotTaken: No deployment, publishing, hosting handoff, or public URL creation
No account creation or sign-in integration
No contact with any person or service
No analytics, notifications, collaboration, or backend setup
No external application assets or runtime network-writing behavior
No repository push or pull request
